codeReview.summary: The re-run only addressed linting issues (commit d4869e1), not the blocking performance and correctness issues from the prior review. All three blocking issues remain unfixed.

issues.description: N+1 query problem in get_subject_stats_for_attempts: Lines 110-124 loop through attempts, then call get_attempt_answers(attempt.id) per attempt, then call get_question_with_topic(answer.question_id) per answer. With 10 attempts x 50 questions = 500+ DB queries. Should use JOINs with selectinload to batch fetch.
N+1 query problem in get_topic_stats_for_attempts: Lines 156-170 have the same nested loop pattern calling get_attempt_answers and get_question_with_topic individually per question.
N+1 query problem in get_weak_questions_for_topic: Lines 194-206 loop through attempts and answers, calling get_question_with_topic individually per incorrect answer.
N+1 query problem in get_time_stats_by_subject: Lines 281-298 loop through attempts and answers, calling get_question_with_topic individually per answer.

Date filtering bug in _calculate_subject_accuracy: When calculating trend (recent vs older stats), if start_date is None (ALL_TIME filter), the code calculates recent_stats with hardcoded 15-day window but older_stats ignores user's date filter entirely (passes None as end_date), producing incorrect trend results. Lines 201-207 and 208-216 need fixing to properly split the user's date range in half for trend comparison.

summary: The re-run (commit d4869e1) only fixed linting issues. All 3 blocking issues from prior review remain unfixed: N+1 queries in 4 repository methods (still using nested loops with individual DB queries), and date filtering bug in subject accuracy trend calculation. Division by zero issue was fixed.
